tokens: We will give you a radar vector on this one.
If you will go over just directly east of Bench Crater; and you can continue on east until you are just about directly opposite the LM.
And then a couple of more steps ought to take you right to Halo Crater.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Sounds like a pretty good vector.
That also says that we are running right into the Sun.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You will be running right into the Sun.
And (when you get near Halo) directly at your 9 o'clock position, you will see the LM.
And then a couple of more steps and you'll be right there.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I've got the LM in sight.
(Pause) You know what I feel like, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Chuckling) Did you ever see those pictures of giraffes running in slow motion?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's exactly what I feel like.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Say, would you giraffes give us some comment on your boot penetration as you move across there - what you're doing now, and what (penetration) you had back there at Sharp Crater?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We don't sink in anywheres near as much.
Now I'm crossing some of my own tracks.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: The toes sink in a bit, Pete, as you push off.
You land flat-footed, so your heels don't sink in; but, as you push off with your toes, they sink in down about 3 inches.
Your heels are only sunk in perhaps an eighth of an inch.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Every time he lands he sends little particles spraying out ahead of him and beside him and everywhere else.
They go out to distances maybe 2 feet to 3 feet around him.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, have we gone too close towards the LM?
(Pause) (We're) going on the south side of Bench Crater, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, if you'll just go directly to the east of the center of Bench Crater and then continue directly east right into the Sun; and then at 9 o'clock, you'll see the LM, and a couple of more steps and you'll be there.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (A little out of breath when talking) Okay.
(Pause) I've got the decided feeling I'm going to sleep tonight.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, the crater you're looking for, Halo Crater, is just about the same size as Sharp Crater and should resemble it.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: (Breathing heavily) I think I have it in sight, but I'm not sure.
There's a couple of them up...I'll tell you what I'm going to do, Houston.
I'm going to take an EMU break.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, the dimension on Halo crater is about 20 feet, so that would make it half of what you saw at Sharp.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Well, Halo...I wonder if I'm standing...You suppose this is it, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, it doesn't have any halo around it.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But you never can tell from here (that is, from the surface).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You can look at the map when you get here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Tell you one thing I'd go for is a good drink of ice water.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Let me look in the map, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete and Al, can we have an EMU check?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And one way to locate it (Halo Crater), also, is that it should be right on the rim of Surveyor crater, and you ought to see Surveyor off directly to the northeast.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Mine reads about 55 percent O2, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Here's a) round glass ball they got to have, Al.
(Pause) Let me have a sample bag.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: First time I've worked up a heart rate, I think.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We got a total of about 5 pounds of rocks.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'd hate to have us get back to the LM and then have to fill it up around there again.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Ah, we're going to the Surveyor Crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think we can get to the bottom of that baby.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why don't you take a rest here?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Do your hands get hot holding that shovel?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: My hands just get hot, period.
I don't know whether it is the shovel or what.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You know, as long as it...Like now, mine are cool.
But the minute I start carrying this tool carrier, they start warming up.
Wouldn't think the thing would be that hot.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Preparing to take a tourist picture) Look over here at me and smile.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll get you; you're right there by a crater.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's ease off at a nice...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I know where we're going now.
I think this is Halo Crater right up here in front of us.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Hey, Ed, you might tell Fred Haise (Apollo 13 LMP) he ought to quit working on running and start working on holding things in his hands for long periods.
My legs don't get a bit tired, but your hands get tired carrying these tools, particularly the Hand Tool Carrier.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Yeah, you wouldn't (think) ...(Stops to listen to Gibson) I think that's funny; you wouldn't think it that way.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Tell Jim Lovell (the Apollo 13 Commander) to practice digging.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, look at all the texturing.
Look here, Pete; now we are crossing across something that's got a completely different texture than what we have been on.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We've run across a sort of a textural contact.
We're suddenly on an area that's quite...(It's) not so smooth; it's got dimples and wrinkles in it.
You want me to take some pictures or what, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Why don't you come up here...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...and we will take a couple of good dirt bag samples of this stuff.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You know, I think this looks like that material that we talked about the first day in front of the LM.
Maybe it runs past the LM down into this area.
But it's sure different than where we've been.
It looks almost (like) it's more...The material is more cohesive and forms clumps, instead of being so nice and smooth.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I was waiting for the gnomon to damp out, but...(Pause)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I wanted to get my footprints in it too, so they can see that.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I think I will take some a little further away.
Back up a little, and shoot a 15-foot (focus) one if it's okay.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I will be back to collect it in just a second; let me get this 15 footer.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (To Houston) Is Halo Crater slightly big(ger than Sharp)...Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I'm shooting about four here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: If you'll get some sample bags and we'll...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Boy, it sure is fine; it's kind of like over at the other...at Sharp Crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Looks the same, except on the surface it just seems...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait a minute and I'll get you another bag full.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: If you saw this on Earth, you would think it was a real soft dirt that it had just been rained on recently.
Not hard rain, but just a sprinkle, so that the droplets (garbled under Pete)
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Now, that's a good sample bag full.
That's 12D, Houston, the sample bag number...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: With a couple or three dimple craters in the south side of it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We can collect a rock while we wait, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Well, look; I think this is Halo Crater right here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...And let's go get some rocks from it and everything.
We're seeing it right...We've actually got the soil sample from part of it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: But this isn't 20 feet in diameter.
Is it right on the rim of the Surveyor crater, Houston?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's affirmative; and, from your comments on the three dimples, we show that you're there.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: What do you want in it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We'd like to get the pan and the double core tube.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I can't believe we're at the right place.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm not sure we're at the right place, either.
Let me look at the top of this hill here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me look at the chart.
There's a nice rock right there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me look at the map.
Not even hardly a crater worth looking at where we are.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: If you see a better location for that double core tube, go ahead.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We're trying to find the right crater, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think it's that area right over there.
Halo is this first one right here, the little one, and then all those others are next over according to the chart.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: So we can just go over there and...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: No; it's right...You see where I'm pointing!
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: As I see it, it's that one right over there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I have the double core tube.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And you want what, Houston, a partial pan?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) We'd like a full pan at that point, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: And also, Al, if you could give us some sort of an estimate of how hard it is to get the core tube in.
That is, what the force history is; how many pounds and how much force.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Hey, look at this little neat-o crater right here.
(Pause) It's a good place to sample.
(Pause) Oh, look at all the glass in the bottom of that baby.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Got a lot of that (in sample bags), though.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I think that's Halo right there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Right over (garbled) that one right there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's take this one right here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Lots of glass down in the bottom of this baby...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete and Al, could we have a readout on the cameras at this point?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You'd better take all these pictures.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, I'd better change cameras because...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: This thing hasn't been taking every picture.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Take a picture and let's see.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I mean, it's been doing it intermittently.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now (garbled) get out and make the double core tube here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, we copy 60 and 110 on the film.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: Here, look at the chart a minute, Pete, while (garbled)...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete and Al, we'd like you to go ahead and get the pans taken on the LMP's camera.
You can either have Al do the pans or switch cameras.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: You'll have to pull the pin and unscrew that if you can.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I'm going to hand you the
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'm not sure that double core tube screws on as far as it should.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Be sure you give us the number of the lower core tube, please.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: The lower core tube is number 3, I think.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Where are you going to drive it?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, let's go over to this crater right here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Boy, it's soft around those little craters.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I can shove it in a little.
I hope this is a good soft place.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Oh, I hit something solid there.
(To Houston) Well, I used all my weight, Houston, and shoved it in about seven inches.
Now, I'll just pound on it a while and see what we can do.
(Pause) It (seems to) be going in okay.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hope we got a good spot.
I don't really think this is the right place.
(Pause) Some of those things aren't so obvious.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me wiggle it a bit.
It's got one core tube completely in now.
This hammer - when you hit on the side of it, like you have to do to hit it within this suit - it knocks little chips of metal off the side of the hammer.
I don't think that's too good.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Is it damaging the hammer or the core tube?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: I'm afraid some of the fragments will damage the suit.
You know, it's just breaking...Hey, I'm better left handed than right.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You even hit it with the front end and some of them pop off.
(Pause) They're flying all over the place.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: He's up to the bottom of the handgrip portion of the upper tube.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It looks like it's got a coating over the hammer, Pete, and I'm knocking the coating.
Instead of being steel or aluminum hammer, it's...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: There is a coating on that hammer and that's probably what you're knocking off.
And also, we want to be sure to get the site there documented.
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: Hit something solid there, didn't you?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's just getting down there, Pete.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, that baby is in the ground.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now the question is can we pull it out?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Garbled) Let me get the down-Sun shot, (that is, a picture taken from the east).
(Pause) I hope that's a good spot.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Ought to get some of these rocks nearby here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Could) you give them a little pan or something, so they can see where this came from?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: I don't have that much film.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why don't I just trade you cameras?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Hey, would you lift mine off when you're through?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I can't pull those things right.
They tend to vacuum weld a little bit or something, I think.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, you hold that one (meaning the LMP camera).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, that (meaning the LMP camera assembly)'s about to come apart.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I'll be darned if it isn't.
I'll fix it; hold the handle there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Well, that kind of bombed out.
I tell you what, the only thing we can...(garbled) is it broke!
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Well, I'll tell you what happened, Houston.
The nut (the thumbwheel on the LMP trigger/handle assembly) that holds the handle of the camera on broke off.
And so, the handle's free, but that's okay.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me ask you a question.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We understand that the nut (thumbwheel) broke, but the camera is still usable, right?
transcript.speaker: Schmitt

tokens: (To Pete) And your nut's loose too, Pete.
Let me tighten it up for you.
Think they work loose in one-sixth g; we knew that, we should have watched it more carefully.
Can you help do that yourself?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Because it's hard for me to do (while Pete's still wearing the CDR camera).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me go put the hammer up (on the handtool carrier) and (put) this (LMP) camera somewhere and (I'll) be right back.
(Pause) There goes the bracket, but that's okay.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But you're going to have to help me get this (CDR) camera off (Pete's RCU) ...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: There's no need to get it off now.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: No; I want to give it to you.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And you're going to use this one?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, you got to take the Surveyor pictures, so why don't I give you the (CDR) camera?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: And you've still got 50 pictures or so.
Make sure it takes a picture each time it turns.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, here, I'll hold it (meaning the LMP camera) and you take this one off.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Garbled) take it off and then I'll tighten the nut.
(Pause) Okay; now, I'm going to (garbled) it down a little bit.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Wait a minute; I'll hold that down.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Push your RCU down a little bit more.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Got to push down some more.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (See if) I can tighten this thing.
(Pause) Okay, I'm tightening it so it'll get tight.
I got enough there; that'll be enough.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Just drop that one (the LMP camera) in here (into the HTC).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Carry it or something (in case they need to use the film in magazine 48).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: But I got too much other stuff (the gnomon and the scoop)
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me carry part of it or something.
Let me go pull out the core tube (which is still in the ground)
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We can always take the magazine off this (that is, off the LMP camera) and put it on the other one (the CDR camera).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's what we can do, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: We just drop it in here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let's go get your core tube.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Take a cap off (the Hand Tool Carrier).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Looking at the damage to the top of the tube) Hey, you sure beat on it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's what it took to get it in the ground.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Straining) I say it's coming up real easy.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Looked for a minute like you were going down real easy.
(Hearty Laughter) The core tube hangs in and your feet just sink down.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, we made a tactical error here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: I think we dropped an end of the tube we shouldn't have dropped.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We got to take them apart.
(which means the lower section still needs a top) Remember?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Well, what we'll have to do is pick it up, right over there.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: It's someplace buried in the dirt.
I'll get it with the...Here, you hold the core tube.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Ah, you got your hose too.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Hey, this ain't going to work, gang.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: See if you can slide that core tube...Wait.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Boy, I drove a nice core tube in there.
Well, it doesn't look any different, though, to the eye - halfway down.
(That is, looking at the bottom of the upper section, the soil doesn't look different from the surface material) Try...(Pause) Loan me the tweezers (means tongs) a moment.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Can you haul the camera, Al?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Why don't I just lift this up?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Well, there goes the shovel (which has just fallen to the ground), but we can get that in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me hold on to that thing.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Pause) Want the shovel back on here (on the extension handle)?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Pete, we copy that you finished the core tube.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We got a double core tube, and all put together correctly.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: No, I'm going to get Al to do that right now.
With the handle off it and everything, by the time we got done handling it, we got dirt all over the lens.
We run out of film; we happen to have another magazine with us, or we could...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...Don't change that; we just took that one off.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Well, we could do that or...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Of course, we don't want to, but if we have to, I guess we can.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What else would you like here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: You're 2 hours and 7 minutes into the EVA.
And we show you leaving Halo at around 2:15.
And, now, that's for a 4-hour EVA.
We've extended you to 30 minutes for a total EVA of 4 hours.
We'd like, before you go on, to have a good EMU check and (figuratively, not literally) sit down and regroup and figure out a plan of attack on the Surveyor.
One thing we would like to make sure is that you remain away from directly below the Surveyor as you move up to it.
That is, move up to it on one side or the other.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: We were talking about it last night.
We're going to approach it from the side.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Probably ought to get one of these rocks here and just throw it in the bag...
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You want to get this one?
Or one (small enough) for a sample bag.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's sample a couple of these laying right over here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Oh, wow; my ears just came back down.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Here, take one quick picture so we can save some film.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Those little holders for these sample bags are ridiculous, you know?
In this light gravity up here, if you put anything in the holder and move, it flips it right out of it.
Come out of there, sample bag.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Give me some of that dirt around there too, Pete.
This is going in sample bag 13D, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, let's move up on the rim of the Surveyor crater and start getting some rocks.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Al, could we have some sample bag numbers while you're working along there?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: I thought I...Didn't I call out 13D, Houston?
I guess I didn't call it out loud enough.
Let me, then, check the next one and if you...The next time we stop, I will tell you the next one for sure and then you will know what it is.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, look at these rocks; they look a little bit different.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: (Pause) Look at that glass in the bottom of that one!
(Pause) They look like granites, don't they?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: They do; they look just like granite.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Here's a beauty over...Here's a beauty!
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let's get this one for sure.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Won't fit in a bag, but it is sure different.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Got a big glass splotch on it.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That gnomon doesn't really damp as fast as it should, you know, Pete.
I think it does great in one g, but one-sixth g, it doesn't seem to damp right.
Let me get the cross-Suns, too.
Oops, got to get over where you are.
We'll just put that in (the HTC unbagged).
(Pause) You be able to scoop it up?
You know you need some tongs that will get bigger samples than (the smaller tongs) we've got.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You know, seeing that, I just thought...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now I want some of these granites over here.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Doesn't that LM look neat, sitting on the other side of that crater?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: We ought to get a shot of that.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me see; how many pictures have I got now, Pete?
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: You're getting close to the end.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: That's 14D, Houston, is the next sample bag; so the last one was 13D.
Let me take a picture quick here.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Al, why don't you step across over here?
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Step across over there (and) photograph that rock right there.
Wait until I drop the gnomon in...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: ...and do it in such a manner as to get this crater that it came out of.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me see if I can.
I'll have to back...Let me get a 15-foot shot.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: That's just what I was just thinking.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Pete, could we have your present position?
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: If you were looking at the Surveyor crater and west was 12 o'clock, we're at the 9 o'clock position on the Surveyor crater.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Copy (that) you're right on the rim.
We'd like to get a good EMU check and a rest here before you proceed.
transcript.speaker: Gibson

tokens: What we are going to do is I'm getting this...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: This one might be good for (Apollo) 13.
Pete, let me reach back here and grab this (parts bag) strap.
(Pause) Okay; now go (down to get the sample).
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Let me roll a little bit over.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Now, if they had a strap like that, they could just hold the other guy while he leaned over and picked up a rock.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: (Looking at the rock he's just picked up) Look at the sheer-face on that rock, something whistled by it or something.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: It's fractured a bit; it's got some pretty interesting fracture marks on it.
It also has got what look like abrasion marks on it.
Boy, there is a lot of flashing crystals in that rock.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Listen; I'll tell you what I recommend we do while we are taking...
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Let me take the picture of wherever the rock was.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: What I recommend we do is change film packs.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Okay, I'll get this camera (that is, the LMP camera) out (to get the partially used magazine).
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Stay right there just a second.
transcript.speaker: Bean

tokens: Shoot a pan, and get the Surveyor.
transcript.speaker: Conrad

tokens: Ah, it's a bad place to shoot, but I'll try it, though.
transcript.speaker: Bean
